$comment: Spec-as-fixture for issue #74: git-guardrails push-target parsing. Each case is run against BOTH harness copies (extensions/git-guardrails.ts and hooks/block-dangerous-git.sh) — verdicts must match (parity gate). 'branch' = the current branch of the repo the command would act on ('' = not a repo / branch unresolvable). 'cwd_branch' vs 'c_path_branch' distinguish the shell cwd repo from a `git -C <path>` target repo.

cases.command: git push origin feat && gh pr create --base main
cases.why: #74 false-positive class 1: greedy regex spans the && chain; the push targets feat, 'main' belongs to gh pr create
#74 false-positive class 2: \bmain\b matches at the hyphen; ref token must EQUAL main/master, not contain it
#74 false-positive class 3: mere co-occurrence of 'push' and 'main' in a non-git command (hit live 3x)
review finding 6: naive newline/separator split manufactured a 'git push origin main' subcommand out of quoted printf text
a ; inside double quotes is data, not a command separator

cases.command: git commit -m "see: git push origin main"
cases.why: quoted commit message stays one token; the git subcommand is commit
inverse fix: quote chars previously stuck to the token ('"main"' != 'main') so a quoted ref slipped the destination check

cases.command: git push origin feat & git push origin main
cases.why: single & (background) is a command separator too; the second command pushes main

cases.command:
cat > /tmp/notes.md <<'EOF'
git push origin main is blocked by the ruleset
EOF
cases.why: #74 false-positive class 3: heredoc body text is not a git invocation

cases.command:
cat <<-EOF
	indented body
	EOF
git push origin main
cases.why: review finding 7: <<- allows a tab-indented terminator; comparing the raw line to the delimiter never matched, so the REAL push after the heredoc was stripped from inspection

cases.command:
cat <<-EOF
	git push origin main
	EOF
cases.why: tab-indented body of a <<- heredoc is still data; terminator must now be recognized so nothing AFTER leaks, but the body itself never blocks

cases.command:
cat <<EOF
	EOF
git push origin main
EOF
cases.why: without the dash, a tab-indented EOF is NOT a terminator in real shells — the push line is heredoc data, matching bash semantics
-C aware: explicit refspec targets feat; -C repo branch is feat
existing behavior preserved: bare push on a feature branch is fine
options before the refspec must be skipped, not treated as refs
deleting a non-default remote branch is routine cleanup per standards
review finding 5: first-token-must-be-git let shell wrappers carry the identical dangerous push
env wrapper, same bypass
a leading VAR=val assignment also made the first token non-git — same class, no wrapper binary needed
wrapper with option + numeric argument before git
wrapper with duration argument before git
wrapper stripping must not change the verdict for safe pushes
echo is not a wrapper — prefix skipping stops at unknown words so quoted-less text stays text
review finding 4: --repo IS the remote (git's repository argument), so 'main' is a refspec — treating --repo as an opaque option pair let 'main' land in the remote slot unchecked
same hole via the --repo=<repo> single-token form
--repo with a feature refspec stays allowed
--repo with no refspec is a bare push — current-branch fallback must still fire
--all pushes EVERY local branch including main; the bare-push fallback only sees the feature branch, so mode flags must block outright
--mirror force-updates or deletes every remote ref, main included — never safe for an agent
--branches is the modern alias for --all
tags cannot move main; only branch-mode flags are inherently main-touching
review finding 10: ':' is git's MATCHING refspec — pushes every branch existing on both sides, main included; empty dst never equaled 'main' so it sailed past the destination check
'+:' force-updates all matching branches — strictly worse than ':'
':dst' with a NON-empty dst is a delete of that ref — only main/master deletion blocks; empty-dst detection must not overreach

cases.command: sudo -u root git push origin main
cases.why: review finding 11: sudo -u consumes a separate argument; 'root' failed every prefix check and the guard bailed → fail-open (the residual documented at #74 close, now fixed)

cases.command: env -u GIT_DIR git push origin main
cases.why: env -u NAME, same bypass — NAME is not an assignment, wrapper, flag, or number

cases.command: doas -u root git push origin main
cases.why: doas -u user, same shape as sudo

cases.command: sudo -u root git push origin feat
cases.why: wrapper-arg skipping must not change the verdict for safe pushes
review finding 8: HEAD is a symbolic ref — on main it pushes main, but the literal-string dst check saw 'HEAD' != 'main' and the nonempty refspec list skipped the current-branch fallback
HEAD on a feature branch pushes the feature branch — must stay allowed
@ is shorthand for HEAD, same symbolic resolution
explicit main destination wins regardless of the symbolic source
destination ref token equals main
src:dst refspec — DESTINATION is main even though source is feat
force push with explicit main destination
existing behavior preserved: bare push resolves to current branch = main
NEW (#74 under-block, Phase G escape 2026-07-13): hook resolved branch from shell cwd (non-repo, ''), not the -C target on main; only the server ruleset (GH006) stopped it
relative -C must resolve against the TOOL-CALL cwd, not the guard process's own cwd — otherwise the wrong repo (or none) is inspected and a main push slips through
same relative -C resolution, feature branch stays allowed

review finding 9: git resolves each -C relative to the directory established by the previous one — overwriting cpath inspected cwd/sub (nonexistent → '' → not-main) instead of cwd/rel-repo/sub on main
chained -C resolution, feature branch stays allowed
-C plus explicit main destination; explicit ref wins regardless of branch

cases.command: git fetch && git push origin main
cases.why: separator-split must still inspect EVERY git-push sub-command, not just the first

cases.command: git push origin feat && git push origin main
cases.why: one blocked sub-command blocks the whole command line (fail-safe)
deleting the default branch ref is a main mutation
master treated identically to main throughout
existing rule preserved
existing rule preserved: feature-branch deletion allowed
existing rule preserved
-C resolution applies to reset --hard too, same under-block shape as push
existing rule preserved: feature branches may hard-reset
always blocked: discards uncommitted work on any branch
review finding 3: literal-space regex missed multi-space/tab separation; tokenizer is whitespace-agnostic
review finding 3: the -- pathspec separator split the dot into its own token past the old regex
same -- separator escape for restore
review finding 3: -f -d as separate tokens evaded the combined -f/-fd patterns
any short-flag cluster containing f is a forced clean
--force long form equals -f
dry run deletes nothing; only forced cleans discard work
inverse fix: the old substring regex ('git checkout \.') false-blocked dotfile pathspecs; only the bare '.' token discards everything
